acceptance_targets: [chapter_10_award_001] Pass if, in the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13-15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55-71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85-95, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157-159, no more than two separate objects or decorations are framed primarily as lovable because they are faulty, and at least one of those moments lands through a different emotional vector that is directly visible on the page. Fail if the sequence still stacks repeated 'wonky but touching' beats as the dominant texture of the day.

[chapter_10_craft_001] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191-205 includes at least two directly rendered bodily or environmental effects of the pressure and no paragraph whose main move is listing what the force is not. Fail if the scene still depends mainly on abstract nouns such as 'condition' or on explanatory negation without new sensory consequence.

[chapter_10_dialogue_001] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:133-145 contains no sentence that verbatim or near-verbatim matches any Amycus example line in outline/style_bible.json, while Duncan still visibly freezes before recovering. Fail if the impression still hinges on 'Which one of you... No volunteers? That's all right' or a close variant.

[chapter_10_prose_001] Pass if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:193-211 uses 'the way...' or equivalent comparison-led framing no more than twice and still clearly communicates pressure, indifference, and thinning wards. Fail if three or more sentences in that span still rely on the same comparative construction.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13-15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55-71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85-95, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157-159, no more than two separate objects or decorations are framed primarily as lovable because they are faulty, and at least one of those moments lands through a different emotional vector that is directly visible on the page. Fail if the sequence still stacks repeated 'wonky but touching' beats as the dominant texture of the day.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps returning to the same emotional device: care expressed through slightly broken, lopsided, or malfunctioning objects. The fairy lights, garland, hand warmer, scarf, and game deck all land on the same 'flawed but endearing' note, so the Christmas tenderness starts to feel curated rather than freshly discovered. That repetition lowers the chapter's award-level surprise and originality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Diversify the emotional textures in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:95, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:159. Keep one or two imperfect-object beats, but let the others land through different pressures such as scarcity, embarrassment, appetite, or relief, and cut at least one narrator gloss that explicitly turns imperfection into charm.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191-205 includes at least two directly rendered bodily or environmental effects of the pressure and no paragraph whose main move is listing what the force is not. Fail if the scene still depends mainly on abstract nouns such as 'condition' or on explanatory negation without new sensory consequence.
findings.problem: The decisive late-night ward-contact scene establishes real physical pressure, then blinks into abstraction and definition-by-negation: 'Not a creature. Not a mind... Just a condition.' The style bible and constitution explicitly forbid euphemistic cosmic vagueness at exactly this kind of moment. Here the chapter explains the threat instead of fully rendering what direct contact feels like, which reads as sanitization/timidity in the climax.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:205 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:211 so Gavin's first direct perception is carried by immediate bodily and environmental consequences before interpretation. Add concrete sensory fallout from the pressure at the window, then let Gavin infer indifference and incompatibility from what his body and the corridor register rather than from a run of abstract labels.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:133-145 contains no sentence that verbatim or near-verbatim matches any Amycus example line in outline/style_bible.json, while Duncan still visibly freezes before recovering. Fail if the impression still hinges on 'Which one of you... No volunteers? That's all right' or a close variant.
findings.problem: Keir's Amycus impression reuses the style bible's calibration-only Amycus line almost verbatim. That is an explicit dialogue-rule breach: example lines are for register calibration only and must never appear in draft text. The moment therefore reads as sourced from notes instead of spoken in scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the quoted wording in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:133 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:145 with new Carrow-like phrasing that keeps the same social effect on Duncan without echoing the style-bible sentence shape or keyword sequence. Preserve the trigger, apology, and fallout, but rebuild the impression from fresh syntax and diction.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:193-211 uses 'the way...' or equivalent comparison-led framing no more than twice and still clearly communicates pressure, indifference, and thinning wards. Fail if three or more sentences in that span still rely on the same comparative construction.
findings.problem: The final scene repeats the same explanatory comparison scaffold over and over: 'the way you might...', 'the way you understand...', 'the way the floor is...', 'the way a river...', 'the way weather...', 'the way you feel...'. Gavin should use ordinary analogies, but this many in one short span becomes a prose tic and flattens the climax into patterned explanation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:193 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:211. Keep at most two comparison-led sentences in that stretch and replace the others with direct sensory statements, harder image structures, or plainer declaratives that do not route every perception through the same 'the way...' template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
